text: Well, good afternoon. I'm going to present the last case of the course.
This case is a right partial nephrectomy performed by Dr. Alberto Breda.

Our current patient is a sixty-seven-year-old female with past medical history of hypertension, paroxysmal tachycardia treated with cardiac ablation, polymyalgia rheumatica, venous insufficiency, and severe scoliosis.

As urological history, the patient's general practitioner performed a CT scan for the neural gauze and an explained left leg edema, which showed an anterior right renal mass of forty-five per forty millimeters at the interpolar slash lower pole region.
The pathway score was ten, renal score is nine. Renal function is normal with the creatinine level of sixty-five micromole per liter.
